content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Construct a permutation P=[P1,P2,...,PN]P = [P_1, P_2, ..., P_N]P=[P1​,P2​,...,PN​] such that there does not exist iii and jjj (1≤i≤j≤N1 \le i \le j \le N1≤i≤j≤N) such that (Pi+Pi+1+...+Pj)mod  (N+1)=0(P_i + P_{i + 1} + ... + P_j) \mod (N + 1) = 0(Pi​+Pi+1​+...+Pj​)mod(N+1)=0. Informally, all subarray sums should not be divisible by (N+1)(N + 1)(N+1).

If it is not possible, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line :



NNN space separated integers representing the permutation PPP if a valid permutation exists.
−1-1−1 if no valid permutation exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 2 : It can be proven that no permutation is valid. For example, consider P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2], then the subarray [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has a sum of 333 which is divisible by (N+1)=3(N + 1) = 3(N+1)=3.
Test case 3 : It can be proven that all subarrays are not divisible by N+1N + 1N+1 for the permutation P=[1,2,3]P = [1, 2, 3]P=[1,2,3]. For example, the subarray [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has a sum of 333 which is not divisible by N+1=4N + 1 = 4N+1=4.
